selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw: {"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"Fake content on social media is a persistent issue that affects both individuals and society as a whole. It can take many forms, including misinformation, disinformation, propaganda, and hate speech. Managing and dealing with fake content requires a multi-faceted approach that involves both technical and non-technical solutions. Here are some strategies that can help:","reasoning_summary":"1. Identify the source of the fake content to determine its credibility. 2. Use fact-checking tools to verify the accuracy of the content. 3. Report suspicious or false content to the platform or account responsible. 4. Engage in respectful and constructive dialogue with those who have created or shared the fake content. 5. Seek out multiple sources of information to get a well-rounded view of the issue.","tone":"informative and empathetic","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"None","used_persona_fields":[]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: Fake content on social media is a persistent issue that affects both individuals and society as a whole. It can take many forms, including misinformation, disinformation, propaganda, and hate speech. Managing and dealing with fake content requires a multi-faceted approach that involves both technical and non-technical solutions. Here are some strategies that can help:
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: 1. Identify the source of the fake content to determine its credibility. 2. Use fact-checking tools to verify the accuracy of the content. 3. Report suspicious or false content to the platform or account responsible. 4. Engage in respectful and constructive dialogue with those who have created or shared the fake content. 5. Seek out multiple sources of information to get a well-rounded view of the issue.
